## Automated Tests Required

### S5-01: iOS Visibility-Change Liveness Probe — Integration

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/chess-engine/visibility-liveness.test.ts`
**Gate level**: BLOCKING — story cannot be marked Complete without passing tests.

**What to test** (from story ACs + `TR-chess-engine-009`):

- **AC-1**: Probe fires after 60s background
  - Mock `Date.now()` to return `lastHeartbeatTs + 61_000`
  - Dispatch `visibilitychange` with `document.hidden = false`
  - Assert: `isready` sent to Worker; 1000ms probe timer started

- **AC-2**: Worker alive when `readyok` arrives within 1000ms
  - Mock Worker sends `readyok` at 500ms (fake timer)
  - Assert: `Worker.terminate()` NOT called; state unchanged

- **AC-3**: Worker respawned when no `readyok` within 1000ms
  - Mock Worker never sends `readyok`; advance fake timer 1001ms
  - Assert: `Worker.terminate()` called; new Worker spawned; UCI handshake re-initiated

- **AC-4**: `requestId` preserved through respawn
  - requestId = 5 at respawn time
  - Stale `bestmove` from old Worker after new Worker starts → dropped by race guard

- **AC-5**: `lastHeartbeatTs` updated on every Worker message
  - Mock Worker sends any UCI message
  - Assert: `lastHeartbeatTs` equals `Date.now()` at time of receipt

**Edge cases to cover** (from chess-engine GDD):
- Background exactly 60_000ms → should NOT trigger probe (threshold = ≥ 60_000ms)
- Multiple rapid `visibilitychange` events → only one probe timer active at a time
- `visibilitychange` fires when Worker already IDLE (no in-flight search) → probe fires; readyok within 1s → no respawn

**Estimated test count**: ~7 unit tests (using `vi.useFakeTimers()`)

---

## Manual QA Checklist

### S5-02: Board Theme — Custom Piece Set & Board Colors

**Verification method**: Screenshot at two viewports + contrast measurement + sign-off
**Who must sign off**: Developer (solo dev signs all roles)
**Evidence doc**: `production/qa/evidence/board-theme-evidence.md`

Checklist:
- [ ] 12 SVG files exist in `src/assets/pieces/` — filenames: `wK wQ wR wB wN wP bK bQ bR bB bN bP`
- [ ] Launch at 375px (iPhone SE preset) — all pieces clearly visible and distinguishable (no clipping)
- [ ] Launch at 1440px (desktop) — pieces clear at standard board size
- [ ] Board squares use 和茶系 palette (warm tones), NOT default green/cream
- [ ] Contrast ≥ 3:1 between light and dark square colors (measure via WebAIM or DevTools)
- [ ] Coordinate labels (if visible) styled consistently with palette
- [ ] `npx vitest run tests/unit/chess-board/` — all existing tests pass (regression check)

*Benchmark: Knight and Bishop must be distinguishable at 375px without zooming.*

---

### S5-03: ADR-0007 Spike (depth-22 reachability)

**Not a standard story — no automated test. Spike result updates ADR-0007.**

Steps:
1. Open app in Chrome; complete a 10-move game
2. Open ReviewView — two-pass analysis runs
3. In DevTools Console, observe logged depths per position
4. Record: typical depth ceiling within `REVIEW_TOTAL_TIME_BUDGET_MS`

Pass: depth 18–22 consistently reached → `REVIEW_TARGET_DEPTH=22` confirmed; update ADR-0007 to remove `provisional`.
Fail: ceiling consistently < 18 → lower `REVIEW_TARGET_DEPTH` in `engine-tuning.ts` and update ADR-0007.

---

### S5-04: Bundle Size Verification

**Evidence doc**: `production/qa/smoke-chess-board-bundle.md`

Checklist:
- [ ] `npm run build` completes without error
- [ ] Identify chess-board JS chunk in `dist/assets/`
- [ ] Gzip size ≤ 120,000 bytes (120 KB)
- [ ] Record exact filename and gzip size in smoke doc
- [ ] If > 120KB: check `manualChunks` in `vite.config.ts` before marking done

---

### S5-05: Memory Budget Verification

**Evidence doc**: `production/qa/evidence/chess-engine-memory-evidence.md`

Checklist:
- [ ] App idle at HomeView → Heap snapshot (baseline)
- [ ] Game in progress (AI thinking) → Heap snapshot → JS heap ≤ 65 MB
- [ ] Post-game ReviewView (both Workers potentially active) → Heap snapshot → ≤ 150 MB hard ceiling
- [ ] If 120–150 MB: PASS but flag advisory (over 120 MB working target)
- [ ] If > 150 MB: FAIL
- [ ] Record all three snapshots (with screenshots) in evidence doc

---

## Smoke Test Scope

Run before QA hand-off:

1. App launches to HomeView without crash or console error
2. New game starts — AI makes a move within 5s
3. Game completes — GAME_OVER overlay appears (New Game + Review buttons)
4. ReviewView loads — two-pass analysis runs; cpLoss displayed
5. **[Sprint 5]** Custom pieces render (not default cburnett set)
6. **[Sprint 5]** App still functional after simulated background → foreground cycle (Chrome tab switch)

---

## Playtest Requirements

No playtest sessions required for Sprint 5. All stories have objective pass/fail criteria or are advisory visual sign-offs.

---

## Definition of Done — Sprint 5

A story is DONE when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] **S5-01** BLOCKING: `tests/unit/chess-engine/visibility-liveness.test.ts` exists; all tests pass; no existing tests broken
- [ ] **S5-02** ADVISORY: `production/qa/evidence/board-theme-evidence.md` exists; screenshots at 375px + 1440px; contrast ratio documented; sign-off table completed
- [ ] **S5-03** ADVISORY: ADR-0007 updated with spike result
- [ ] **S5-04** ADVISORY: `production/qa/smoke-chess-board-bundle.md` exists; chunk ≤ 120KB documented
- [ ] **S5-05** ADVISORY: `production/qa/evidence/chess-engine-memory-evidence.md` exists; all three heap snapshots recorded
- [ ] Full test suite passes: `npx vitest run` — no regressions
- [ ] `production/session-state/active.md` updated at sprint close
